title: mycelis muralis
intro: Mycelis muralis, also known as Lady's Purse or Woodland Penny-cress, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The leaves and flowers of Mycelis muralis have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Mycelis muralis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for those seeking natural health solutions.
